title: Salmon Wrap with Yogurt Herb Sauce
description: Wild salmon, crisp vegetables, and a Greek yogurt herb sauce with dill, capers, and lemon — wrapped in a whole grain tortilla or romaine leaf. High protein, rich in omega-3s.

ingredients.name: whole grain wraps (or large romaine leaves for a lettuce wrap)

instructions.text: Make the yogurt herb sauce: combine yogurt, dill, lemon juice, garlic, Dijon mustard, and capers in a small bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Stir well and refrigerate while you cook the salmon. Keeps in the fridge for 3 days.
Pat salmon dry and season both sides with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Place salmon skin-side up and cook without moving it, 3–4 minutes until golden.

Flip salmon and cook until just opaque through the center — it should flake easily but still look slightly glossy in the middle. Remove from heat and rest. Flake into large chunks when cool enough to handle.
If using whole grain wraps, warm them briefly in a dry skillet over low heat for about 30 seconds per side to keep them pliable.

Spread a generous layer of yogurt herb sauce across the center of each wrap. Layer arugula or romaine first (it acts as a moisture barrier), then tomato, cucumber, red onion, avocado, and finally the flaked salmon. Fold in the sides, roll tightly from the bottom, and slice on a diagonal.

notes:
Wild salmon at 6 oz delivers roughly 2–3g EPA+DHA omega-3s — one of the two fatty fish servings per week most guidelines recommend.

Lettuce wrap option: a large romaine leaf works well as a wrap alternative and keeps it fully grain-free.

Batch tip: cook extra salmon — leftover flaked salmon over arugula with the same yogurt sauce makes a solid lunch the next day with no additional prep.

The yogurt herb sauce is worth making a double batch — it works on fish, grain bowls, and as a vegetable dip.
